story: As a listener, I want to browse every track in the archive, newest release first.
As a listener, I want to filter tracks by release year, tempo, key, label, and galaxy.
As a listener, I want to see what just came out in drum & bass.
As a listener, I want to search the whole archive by name, coordinate, or a plain-language query.
As a listener, I want to find tracks that sound like one I already love.
As a listener, I want to open any track or mixtape by its Log ID coordinate.
As a listener, I want to browse every drum & bass artist, A to Z.
As a listener, I want an artist's profile: bio, releases, and where to follow them.
As a listener, I want artists similar to one artist I like.
As a listener, I want to multi-select artists I like and see who sounds like all of them.
As a fan, I want to follow one artist's new releases.
As a listener, I want to browse every album and EP, A to Z.
As a listener, I want an album's page: tracklist, artists, label.
As a listener, I want to browse every record label, A to Z.
As a listener, I want a label's page: its releases, artists, and lineage.
As a fan, I want to follow one label's new releases.
As a DJ, I want to chain a set: seed my taste and have the engine rank what mixes in clean next.
As a DJ, I want to share a set as a link and have anyone reopen it.
As a developer or agent, I want a documented machine interface to the archive.
As a user, I want to check whether Fluncle's services are up.
As a fan, I want to follow Fluncle's latest finds as he logs them.
As a fan, I want to read why Fluncle logged a track — the note, the video, the observation.
As a listener, I want the archive to throw me a random banger.
As a fan, I want to swipe findings as full-bleed video stories.
As a fan, I want to lean back and listen to Fluncle's spoken observations on a shared clock.
As a signed-in fan, I want personal recommendations grown from my own taste.
As a listener, I want to browse the archive by how it sounds, not what it's called.
As a fan, I want to play the Galaxy — fly to every banger's star.
As a player, I want my Galaxy progress to follow my account across devices.
As a fan, I want to browse Fluncle's own DJ mixtapes.
As a fan, I want to experience a mixtape — the set video, the tracklist, the ride.
As a fan, I want to read the voyage — Fluncle's travelogue, one entry per sector-day.
As a newcomer, I want to learn who Fluncle is and what this place is.
As a curious visitor, I want a Log ID explained to me.
As a curious fan, I want to see Fluncle's reach across platforms over time.
As a crew member, I want to talk with Fluncle about drum & bass.
As a crew member, I want to send Fluncle a track I think he'd log.
As a fan, I want fresh finds in my inbox every Friday.
As a fan, I want an account that follows me across surfaces.
As a signed-in fan, I want to save findings for later.
As a DJ, I want to save the sets I build to my account.
As a fan, I want a push when a new finding or mixtape lands.
